scope_summary: Contains the WAPC's general criteria and design standards for the provision of school and TAFE college sites in residential areas of Western Australia, applied during district/local structure planning and subdivision design for new residential subdivisions. It covers demand ratios for school sites, location within catchments, site size and physical requirements, site selection, access and road safety, relationships to nearby land uses, children's services facilities and consultation with education authorities.

key_numeric_standards.applies_to: government primary schools in new residential areas

key_numeric_standards.topic: combined primary and secondary school site size
additional site area for children's services facilities
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: school sites with associated children's services facilities

key_numeric_standards.topic: Catholic primary school site size where church/community facilities included
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: Catholic Education Commission primary school sites

key_numeric_standards.topic: reduced primary school site size where co-located with public open space
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: primary schools co-located with POS under management agreements

key_numeric_standards.topic: minimum feasible TAFE college site / optimal size for growth

quality_flags: vertical sidebar banner text extracted as one-character-per-line strings at page starts ('SCHOOL SITES POLICY NO. DC 2.4')
two-column PDF text interleaved in places (headings merged across columns)
missing spaces in some scanned-era phrases (e.g. '1992for', 'Codewhich')

policy dates from October 1998 and predates Liveable Neighbourhoods finalisation and current structure-planning framework; treat numeric standards as historical guidance unless confirmed still applied
